name: Neuro-Linguistic Programming (Communication Model)
description: Communication and behavioral patterns that shape how language influences thinking and outcomes.
when_to_use: Agent needs to reframe a problem for the user
Task requires persuasive or motivating communication
Agent needs to calibrate communication style to the user
Shifting perspective is needed to unlock a solution

reframing.description: Changing the meaning of a situation by changing the frame around it

context_reframe.description: The same behavior/trait is valuable in a different context
context_reframe.template: That [trait/behavior] would be really valuable when [different context]
context_reframe.example: Being extremely detail-oriented feels slow for brainstorming, but it's exactly what's needed for quality assurance

meaning_reframe.description: Assign a different meaning to the same situation
meaning_reframe.template: Instead of meaning [negative], this could mean [positive/neutral]
meaning_reframe.example: The client's pushback doesn't mean rejection — it means they care enough to engage deeply

agent_prompts: What's another way to interpret this situation?
In what context would this 'weakness' be a strength?
What meaning is the user assigning to this, and is there a more useful one?

meta_model.description: Precise questions to challenge vague, distorted, or generalized language

generalization.description: One experience becomes a universal rule
challenges: Always? Has there ever been an exception?

examples: 'If I do X, Y will definitely happen'
challenges: How specifically did they cause that feeling?
How do you know what they think? Have you asked?
What evidence supports that Y will definitely happen?

anchoring.description: Associating a positive state with a specific trigger for future access
anchoring.agent_application: When the user achieves a win or breakthrough, anchor it — reference it in future conversations to restore that confident state
protocol: Identify a peak state (confidence, clarity, motivation)
Name it and make it vivid ('Remember when you [specific achievement]?')
Create a reference point the user can return to
In future sessions, recall the anchor when the user needs that state

questions: What specifically do you want? (stated positively, not what you DON'T want)
How will you know when you have it? (sensory evidence)
Where, when, and with whom do you want it?
What will you gain AND what might you lose?
What resources do you already have?
